title: Has De-Dollarization Begun?
content: US President Donald Trump’s military adventures, attacks on long-standing allies, and dismantling of institutions like USAID are eroding the trust on which the dollar’s global primacy ultimately rests. The world’s reserve currency may have already begun its long, slow decline.
snippet: US President Donald Trump’s military adventures, attacks on long-standing allies, and dismantling of institutions like USAID are eroding the trust on which the dollar’s global primacy ultimately rests
creator: Kaushik Basu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A US-India Partnership for the AI Age
content: Although the United States maintains a decisive edge in frontier AI models and high-end compute, China has proven that its capacity for innovation is a force to be reckoned with. To prevent the Chinese government from claiming the strategic high ground, the US needs the talent and data that only India can provide.
snippet: Although the United States maintains a decisive edge in frontier AI models and high-end compute, China has proven that its capacity for innovation is a force to be reckoned with. To prevent the Chines
creator: Ylli Bajraktari
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: After Hungary, Could Mexico Be Next?
content: During his 16-year rule, Hungary's Viktor Orbán could plausibly argue that his model delivered order, stability, and rising living standards. But Mexico's government can make no such claim, suggesting that the country might find itself facing a destabilizing political transition sooner than expected.
snippet: During his 16-year rule, Hungary's Viktor Orbán could plausibly argue that his model delivered order, stability, and rising living standards. But Mexico's government can make no such claim, suggesting
creator: Guillermo Ortiz
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: How Many Divisions Has Pope Leo?
content: Following the Trump administration's spat with Pope Leo XIV over its misbegotten war with Iran, one is reminded of previous historical episodes when the Vatican offered moral clarity for a world that had been corrupted by cynicism and aggressive authoritarianism. Americans, above all, should heed the message.
snippet: Following the Trump administration's spat with Pope Leo XIV over its misbegotten war with Iran, one is reminded of previous historical episodes when the Vatican offered moral clarity for a world that
creator: Harold James
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What China Will Learn from Orbán’s Defeat
content: After watching Nikita Khrushchev’s repudiation of Stalin lead to a revolution in Hungary, Mao Zedong concluded that under one-party rule, no amount of political liberalization is safe. The defeat of Viktor Orbán in Hungary’s parliamentary elections last month most likely reinforced that lesson for Chinese President Xi Jinping.
snippet: After watching Nikita Khrushchev’s repudiation of Stalin lead to a revolution in Hungary, Mao Zedong concluded that under one-party rule, no amount of political liberalization is safe. The defeat of V
creator: Sam Chetwin George
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Real Private-Credit Risk Is Opacity, Not Leverage
content: Credit risk is migrating beyond the traditional banking system into a sprawling network of funds, insurers, and lenders, with no single authority able to see the whole picture. The challenge facing policymakers is to ensure that risks remain visible as they shift into corners of the financial system not designed to absorb them.
snippet: Credit risk is migrating beyond the traditional banking system into a sprawling network of funds, insurers, and lenders, with no single authority able to see the whole picture. The challenge facing po
creator: Amit Seru
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: A New Economics for the 21st Century
content: By coming out in favor of industrial policy after many decades of advising against government intervention in the economy, the World Bank has taken an important step. But it still has much further to go to translate real-world evidence into better policy advice.
snippet: By coming out in favor of industrial policy after many decades of advising against government intervention in the economy, the World Bank has taken an important step. But it still has much further to
creator: Mariana Mazzucato
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: An Economic Model for Securing Hormuz
content: With a military solution to the closure of the Strait unlikely to succeed, the only sustainable long-term arrangement is to institutionalize today’s emerging arrangement, by which Iran, in coordination with the Gulf states, guarantees safe passage for a fee. Such a system could benefit all parties—not least the United States.
snippet: With a military solution to the closure of the Strait unlikely to succeed, the only sustainable long-term arrangement is to institutionalize today’s emerging arrangement, by which Iran, in coordinatio
creator: Massoud Karshenas
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Who Lost America’s Universities?
content: Lost in all the debates about whether US higher education is serving its proper function are conservative critics’ own motives. Leveraging the public’s apparent loss of confidence in colleges and universities, the American right has shifted the discussion from academic freedom to free speech, using the latter to undermine the former.
snippet: Lost in all the debates about whether US higher education is serving its proper function are conservative critics’ own motives. Leveraging the public’s apparent loss of confidence in colleges and univ
creator: Jan-Werner Mueller
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Beware of Government by AI
content: As countries like the United Arab Emirates increasingly delegate decision-making to algorithms, often with little transparency, the risks are no longer hypothetical. Evidence shows these systems can cause widespread harm, erode democratic accountability, and concentrate power in the hands of tech titans.
snippet: As countries like the United Arab Emirates increasingly delegate decision-making to algorithms, often with little transparency, the risks are no longer hypothetical. Evidence shows these systems can c
creator: Gabriela Ramos
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Bigger Defense Budgets Are Not Enough to Secure Europe
content: Contrary to popular belief, the main obstacle to Europe’s strategic autonomy is not a lack of funding but its failure to develop critical technologies. Overcoming this constraint will require a fundamentally different approach to investment that better aligns public and private capital.
snippet: Contrary to popular belief, the main obstacle to Europe’s strategic autonomy is not a lack of funding but its failure to develop critical technologies. Overcoming this constraint will require a fundam
creator: Paolo Surico
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Preventing Online Child Abuse Should Be Easier
content: The international response to online child abuse continues to fall short. The problem is not that we lack known solutions or sufficient evidence to understand the challenge; it is that we have failed to apply existing tools fully, consistently, and in a globally coordinated fashion.
snippet: The international response to online child abuse continues to fall short. The problem is not that we lack known solutions or sufficient evidence to understand the challenge; it is that we have failed
creator: Andy Phippen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Living Without an AI Kill Switch
content: With the arrival of Anthropic’s scarily powerful Mythos model, the tech titans that have long argued against any kind of AI regulation are now clamoring for it. Governments should take immediate steps to coordinate effectively and enable rapid information sharing, while also compelling developers to meet basic safety standards.
snippet: With the arrival of Anthropic’s scarily powerful Mythos model, the tech titans that have long argued against any kind of AI regulation are now clamoring for it. Governments should take immediate steps
creator: Ngaire Woods
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Sun Tzu’s Advice for Donald Trump
content: Amid a series of massive, illegal policy blunders, US President Donald Trump has set his sights on stabilizing relations with Chinese President Xi Jinping at their May summit. But it will probably go poorly because Trump is incapable of understanding the importance of strategy, a lesson that China's leaders have long grasped.
snippet: Amid a series of massive, illegal policy blunders, US President Donald Trump has set his sights on stabilizing relations with Chinese President Xi Jinping at their May summit. But it will probably go
creator: Stephen S. Roach
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The EU Needs New Security Partners
content: Europe’s security order needs to be reconstituted. In a world of fracturing alliances, closer security partnerships with a wider range of states, however small, will increasingly become a source of power, allowing Europe to defend its interests wherever they are at stake.
snippet: Europe’s security order needs to be reconstituted. In a world of fracturing alliances, closer security partnerships with a wider range of states, however small, will increasingly become a source of po
creator: Nicu Popescu
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: Asia’s Economic Diplomacy for Tumultuous Times
content: Instead of allowing external powers to dictate their development, China, India, Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam have balanced strategic international engagement with domestic capacity-building and decision-making. This model has become newly relevant for Canada, the United Kingdom, and other middle powers.
snippet: Instead of allowing external powers to dictate their development, China, India, Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam have balanced strategic international engagement with domestic capacity-building and dec
creator: Ashok Lavasa
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: What’s Wrong With the SAVE America Act?
content: Much of the discussion about US President Donald Trump’s proposed SAVE America Act has centered on the bill’s partisan electoral implications. But given the inevitability of false positives, Americans should be asking a more fundamental question: Am I willing to give up my own right to vote to keep a non-citizen from voting?
snippet: Much of the discussion about US President Donald Trump’s proposed SAVE America Act has centered on the bill’s partisan electoral implications. But given the inevitability of false positives, Americans
creator: John Mark Hansen
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: South Korea’s Precarious Balancing Act
content: Caught between US President Donald Trump’s unpredictable administration and a rising China, South Korea is highly exposed to geopolitical turmoil and energy shocks. With American reliability in doubt, it must now safeguard its export-driven economy and build its own capabilities while navigating an increasingly unstable world.
snippet: Caught between US President Donald Trump’s unpredictable administration and a rising China, South Korea is highly exposed to geopolitical turmoil and energy shocks. With American reliability in doubt,
creator: Anne O. Krueger
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: China Needs Higher Minimum Wages
content: Chinese policymakers long viewed workers’ grueling schedules as a byproduct of industrial catch-up. But this has become untenable: if Chinese workers are to become middle-class consumers and build larger families, they cannot be spending most of their waking hours chasing overtime pay, only to return exhausted to dormitories.
snippet: Chinese policymakers long viewed workers’ grueling schedules as a byproduct of industrial catch-up. But this has become untenable: if Chinese workers are to become middle-class consumers and build lar
creator: Xin Meng
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The High Cost of Trump’s Crony Diplomacy
content: Effective diplomacy depends on credibility, consistency, and a clear alignment with national interests. The US administration’s personalized, opaque, and venal shadow diplomacy delivers none of that, and it will leave the US less respected, less trusted, and less effective on the world stage.
snippet: Effective diplomacy depends on credibility, consistency, and a clear alignment with national interests. The US administration’s personalized, opaque, and venal shadow diplomacy delivers none of that,
creator: Brahma Chellaney
sourceDescription: Project Syndicate - The World’s Opinion Page

title: The Universal Uprising
content: Rather than a story of passive suffering, the history of slavery in the Americas is one of rebellion, solidarity, and democratic invention. A recent book traces the depth and creativity of enslaved people’s resistance, whose legacy continues to inspire social-justice movements around the world.

creator: Jake Subryan Richards
